logic: Builds reverse adjacency from edges (controlSuccessors), runs DFS cycle detection via detectCycles(). For each cycle found, emits a PathErr for every consecutive edge pair in the cycle (skipping self-loops).
error_message: line connections do not allow parallel lines to intersect and form loops with each other
description: Detects cycles in the workflow graph. Each edge in a cycle produces a PathErr between the two connected nodes.

logic: For entry (start) nodes, checks that outDegree > 0 (the start node has at least one outgoing edge).
description: Validates that the start node is connected to at least one downstream node via an outgoing edge.

logic: For selector/branch nodes (nodes with expected ports from BranchAdaptor or error-handling exception branches), checks that every expected port has at least one outgoing edge. Accumulates missing ports into one message.
description: Validates that every port on a selector/branch node (including error-handling exception branch ports) has at least one outgoing connection. Missing ports are concatenated into a single message.

logic: For non-selector, non-entry, non-exit, non-break, non-continue nodes, checks that outDegree > 0.
description: Validates that a regular (non-branch, non-terminal) node has at least one outgoing connection. Break and Continue nodes are excluded from this check.

logic: When a BlockInput value is of type Ref and source is BlockOutput, checks that the BlockID field is not empty.
description: Validates that a block output reference has a non-empty block ID. Guards against malformed variable references.

logic: When a BlockInput value references a block output (source=BlockOutput), checks that the referenced block ID exists among the combined reachable nodes (current scope + parent scopes).
error_message: the node id "%s" on which node id "%s" depends does not exist
description: Validates that the node referenced by a variable dependency actually exists in the reachable graph. Catches dangling references to deleted or unreachable nodes.

logic: For every InputParameter on reachable nodes, validates the parameter name against regex ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ (must start with letter/underscore, followed by alphanumeric/underscore only).
error_message: parameter name only allows number or alphabet, and must begin with alphabet, but it's "%s"
description: Validates input parameter naming conventions. Parameter names must be valid identifiers: start with a letter or underscore, contain only alphanumeric characters and underscores.

logic: Iterates reachable nodes. If a node has nested reachability (it is a composite node like batch/loop), and that nested reachability itself contains further nested reachability (i.e., doubly nested composite nodes), emits an error.
error_message: composite nodes such as batch/loop cannot be nested
description: Prevents nesting of composite nodes (batch, loop) inside other composite nodes. Only one level of nesting is allowed.

logic: For VariableAssigner nodes, compares the type of each assigned variable against the global variable metadata (app-level or agent-level). If the types differ, emits an error.
description: Validates that variable assignments match the declared type of the corresponding global variable. Catches type mismatches between local assignment and global variable definition.

logic: When both the global variable and the local assignment are array types, compares the element types. If the array element types differ, emits an error.
error_message: node name %v, param [%s], array element type mismatch
description: Validates that array-type global variable assignments have matching element types. Extends the type mismatch check to array element level.

logic: Collects all sub-workflow nodes, resolves their referenced workflow canvases (by draft ID or versioned ID). If the referenced workflow canvas cannot be found in the resolved map, emits an error.
error_message: sub workflow has been modified, please refresh the page
description: Validates that a sub-workflow reference resolves to an existing workflow. Catches stale references after the sub-workflow has been deleted or modified.

logic: When the sub-workflow canvas is found, retrieves its end node and compares the end node's TerminatePlan with the parent node's TerminationType (mapped via toTerminatePlan). If they differ, the sub-workflow has been modified incompatibly.
error_message: sub workflow has been modified, please refresh the page
description: Validates that the sub-workflow's termination plan matches what the parent workflow expects. Catches cases where the sub-workflow's end behavior changed after initial linking.
